author: @NeoShameMan
text: I watch a bunch of people doing 3 to 7hours video on a subject, and their comment are full of people as excited as me about it.

author: @lowrads
text:
There's also a network effect whereby successful producers refer viewers to one another, thereby chaining their interests.

My nieces and nephews can hardly stay focused on standard television because it's not interactive.  They've been conditioned from very young ages to expect this.  I doubt that will change over time.

author: @AndreaDingbatt
text:
Thank you Robert,
I have been thinking about this for quite some time now, and I am really keen on getting to work, so Thank You very much for your kindness in the information you give us.
And you are definitely interesting to watch and listen to!!
Your community here is very much one of the best!!
I have been having a few issues with my health, which has now become a depression, so I look forward to seeing your videos, because you never fail to inspire and cheer me up!
Kindest Regards,
Andrea, Jasper and George the Pigeon. XxX.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: I did wonder Andrea and blew your heart and kindness - cheers

author: @ladamurni
text: Hardly anyone watches my video's. If you look at some Indonesian Youtubers, lousy video's made with a mobile phone hardly any usefull content. But they have thousends of subscribers in just a few months and every video has at least a 1000 views. Why did I invest in a good camera and spend even more time on editing then recording content. What am I doing wrong? Should I also make video's with my mobile phone about nothing?

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: I hope you don't take this as a criticism as I really do mean to try and help but it would seem your videos are in Dutch - I did take a look at a couple. It might be you have narrowed your possible audience too much after all there are only 25 million native Dutch speakers in the world and Indonesian has 273 million speakers so you are only able to capture 10% of what an Indonesian speaker can

replies.author: @ladamurni
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering Thank you for reaction! I see what I will do with it. The point is that a lot of dutchman do their video's in english, they forget that not everyone in the Netherlands speaks it. And it is a bit weird for a dutchman to listen to another dutchman speaking english witch a weird accent. But maybe I will have to do the same altough it is not my preference. Thank you and have a nice day!

author: @jonnyswalk_7
text:
Well put Rob 👏 Thanks for that 👍 You have a powerful knack of sometimes explaining complex things in a succinct and understandable way. Also apart from all the stuff that you explain and demonstrate (often in a passionate and entertaining manner) one thing that you didn’t mention (I’m sure for the sake of humility 😉) is that people like people (with personality) and often thats another reason why folk follow/subscribe to channels - it’s almost like going over your interesting mates house to see what he’s up to. 
So at the risk of making us all a little embarrassed, we like YOU (and Luke by the way too) as well as what you do and thats another reason we keep coming back. 
I reckon you’ve either got that or you haven’t. 
If there was a one-dimensional lab inmate laboriously monotonically explaining the exact same “clever”stuff, I know I’d have trotted on yonks ago! 
So to sum up thank you for being you mate…(and Luke) 👏 
I’ve learned a lot and been entertained along the way 👍 
Warmest wishes from South Wales 🏴󠁧󠁢󠁷󠁬󠁳󠁿 Jonathan 😊 👍

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: cheers mate and I think you are right 'style' of presentation has a big impact - nice point cheers
